title: Product Validation Schema
description: Gate-1 readiness: each product pre-declares its promise, distributor, hypotheses, and success criteria. This schema defines what those declarations must include. The readiness criteria (gate-readiness/criteria.json) then audit against this filled schema.

last_updated.description: ISO 8601 date this validation was last updated

validated_by.description: The person or process that validated this product

one_line_pitch.description: The promise in one sentence, max 140 chars. Answers: what does the user GET that they couldn't before?

promise_statement.description: The full promise (3-5 sentences). What the product delivers, for whom, and why it matters.

distributor.description: Distributor hypothesis: who sells this, and how they benefit

archetype.description: The named distributor type (e.g. 'singing teachers & academies', 'accelerators', 'research & CX firms'). NOT 'SMBs' or generic.

hypothesis.description: The distributor's exact value prop: what does this product let them do or sell?

pain_point_solved.description: The concrete pain the distributor's current workflow has. This is the friction the product removes.

go_to_market.description: How does the distributor offer this? White-label = their brand only; co-branded = both; powered-by = our brand visible; standalone = direct consumer; internal = no distributor.

end_user.description: End-user hypothesis: who actually uses the product, and what they want

job_to_be_done.description: What job does the user hire this product to do? (e.g. 'practise my pitch out loud and get specific feedback')

friction_before.description: What is the current friction/pain the end-user faces? (e.g. 'I can't hear if my pitch lands until I'm in the room')

success_moment.description: The moment the user feels the product worked: the 'I want that' / 'wow' instant.

friction_point.description: The core friction this product exists to remove

statement.description: A one-sentence articulation of the friction.

today_workaround.description: How do users solve this TODAY? (manual, external tool, doesn't exist, etc.)

why_it_matters.description: Why is this friction worth fixing? (cost, speed, quality loss, risk, etc.)

success_criteria.description: What does success look like? 3-5 measurable outcomes the product must deliver.

evidence.description: How will we know if this is true? What is the proof?

phase.description: When must this be demonstrated? Gate-1 thin MVP, Gate-2 full build, or live?

promise_attributes.description: The product's core promises: each with a quality bar that is measurable and non-negotiable.

attribute.description: The named promise (e.g. 'Karaoke / sing-along', 'Knows your voice', 'White-label')

quality_bar.description: The specific, measurable quality bar. NOT vague. (e.g. 'real backing audible IN-EAR with latency under 60ms' not 'high quality audio'). This is the bar a naive-tester uses to verify it actually works.

how_verified.description: How is this verified? NAIVE=human walkthrough, AUTO=automated check, JUDGE=subjective judgment, VOICE=voice-auditor

gate_readiness_relevance.description: IN=must be present in thin MVP, IN-core=the central promise, COND=only if feature exists, SEC=security/always, SCALE=post-Gate-2, DEFER=polish

essential.description: If true, absence of this attribute = hard no on Gate-1. If false, it's important but not blocking.

commitment_surface.description: How does the product ship? What does the distributor (and end-user) need to commit to using it?

supported.description: Can users run this on their own infrastructure/keys?

what_you_supply.description: What does the user need to provide? (e.g. 'your own ElevenLabs key', 'Anthropic API key', 'Supabase project')

setup_time_minutes.description: How long to get running with your own keys? (target for Gate-1)

documentation.description: Link to or summary of setup docs

output_format.description: What does the user get back? How do they integrate it?

primary_output.description: What does the product produce? (e.g. 'MP3 file', 'API response', 'dashboard', 'PDF report')

format_details.description: Specifics: schema, size, latency, reproducibility (e.g. 'JSON with voice + text transcript, <100ms latency')

pilot_path.description: How does a distributor / end-user get started?

minimum_viable_pilot.description: What's the smallest real use the distributor can run in Week 1? (not 'sign up and explore')

success_signal.description: How does the distributor know the pilot is working? (e.g. 'student completes 1 session and rates coach 4+/5')

time_to_value_days.description: How many days until distributor sees tangible value?

gate_scores.description: Readiness measurement: how well does this product meet the Gate-1 criteria?

hard_gates.description: Criteria that MUST pass (no exceptions). If any fail, Gate-1 is blocked.

evidence.description: What shows this passes (link to PR, screenshot, deploy URL, etc.)

waiver_reason.description: If waived, why? (logged decision, not a silent skip)

weighted_gates.description: Criteria with weights. Aggregate score: sum(weight) for passed / sum(all weight). Target: 80%+.

conditional_gates.description: Criteria that apply only IF a certain feature is present (e.g. auth, voice, email). If feature absent, N/A.

feature_present.description: Does the triggering feature exist in this product?

weighted_score_percent.description: Weighted gates: (sum of weights passed / sum of all weights) * 100. Target: 80%.

gate1_ready.description: Overall verdict: all hard gates pass AND weighted score >= 80%?

fix.description: What needs to happen to pass?

open_items.description: Failed/pending criteria and their required fixes

product.name: SayFix
product.one_line_pitch: Record your speech, hear exactly what you sound like, get coached to fix it.
product.promise_statement: SayFix captures how you speak, plays it back with real-time annotations (filler words, pace, clarity issues), and coaches you through specific improvements. For public speakers, interviewees, and professionals who need to sound confident in the moment.

distributor.archetype: Public speaking coaches & corporate training firms
distributor.hypothesis: These coaches can offer a higher-value service: feedback that's specific, consistent, and instant—without needing the coach to listen to every take. Their students get better faster, and the coach scales across more students.
distributor.pain_point_solved: Coaches spend 30% of their time listening to recordings and giving generic feedback. Students record, email, wait, then get vague notes ('slower', 'clearer'). SayFix lets feedback be instant and specific, freeing the coach to coach, not listen.

end_user.persona: Public speaking students & professionals (sales, interview prep, executives)
end_user.job_to_be_done: I need to know what my voice actually sounds like when I practice, and I need specific things to fix before my big moment.
end_user.friction_before: I can't hear myself clearly when I'm speaking. The playback is always shocking because I didn't know I said 'um' 20 times. My coach's notes come days later and say 'speak slower'—not specific enough to actually change.
end_user.success_moment: I hit record, listen back, and immediately see '0:15 filler word cluster', '0:43 dropped ending', '1:22 rushed pacing'. I redo that 30 seconds, hear the difference, and think 'wow, I actually sound prepared.'

friction_point.statement: Speakers don't hear their own speech clearly until it's too late, and feedback is generic or slow.
friction_point.today_workaround: Record on phone, listen in your car, ask a friend, or pay a coach to listen to every take.
friction_point.why_it_matters: Poor delivery undermines good ideas. Speakers who sound confident get funded, hired, promoted. Practicing without real feedback wastes hours.

success_criteria.criterion: Student completes 5 practice sessions in first week with SayFix (proof: session count + timestamp)
success_criteria.evidence: Dashboard shows usage > 5 sessions in Week 1

success_criteria.criterion: Student rates the feedback as 'specific and actionable' (not generic) in a post-session 1-5 scale (4+ = success)
success_criteria.evidence: In-app survey: mean score >= 4.0 across 10 test users

success_criteria.criterion: Coach sees measurable student improvement: recorded clarity score improves session 1 → session 5
success_criteria.evidence: Coach dashboard shows clarity metric improvement >= 10% over 5 sessions

success_criteria.criterion: Coach can deploy a white-label instance in <2 hours with their branding
success_criteria.evidence: Deployment script documented + tested with a sample coach

promise_attributes.quality_bar: Recording plays back instantly with overlaid annotations (filler words, pace issues, clarity gaps) on the timeline. Not a post-session report. Annotations update in <2s of playback.
Coach sees per-session feedback (e.g., '0:43 you said um 3x in 10s' not 'work on filler words'). Feedback references exact timestamps and counts, not vibes.
System shows clarity/pace/filler score trending upward across sessions for the same user. After 5 takes, a user should see a 10%+ improvement in at least one metric.
Coach's logo, domain, and colors visible throughout. No 'powered by SayFix'. Branding script takes <1h to configure.

Optional: voice agent prompts next coaching step proactively after a session (e.g., 'I heard 5 filler words—let's do a focused take on that section'). Not required for Gate-1, but present if voice is enabled.

run_on_your_data.what_you_supply: Your own Anthropic API key (for analysis) + ElevenLabs key (if voice coach enabled)

output_format.primary_output: Annotated audio playback + scored feedback report
output_format.format_details: MP3 + JSON feedback schema with timestamps and metric scores. <500ms latency from upload to first playback. Reproducible: same audio = same score each run.
integrations: Slack (coach notification: student hit clarity target)

pilot_path.minimum_viable_pilot: Coach onboards 1 student, that student records 2 practice sessions, coach reviews feedback. Real usage, not a sandbox.
pilot_path.success_signal: Student completes 2 sessions + rates feedback >=4/5. Coach confirms: 'I can see what to fix.'

hard_gates.check: Named distributor archetype on the card (not 'SMBs')
hard_gates.evidence: Card states: 'Public speaking coaches & corporate training firms'
BUSINESS_MODEL.md §3 questions answered with distributor/end-user/friction all named
Tested in /browse at 375px and 1440px; no horizontal scroll; playback controls thumb-reachable

hard_gates.check: No secrets in committed files / logs
hard_gates.evidence: Ran cso daily scan; no secrets flagged

weighted_gates.evidence: Landing page header: 'Record your speech, get instant real-time feedback, improve your delivery.' Empty state: 'No sessions yet. Upload or record your first take.'
Hero video shows user recording → playback with annotations → immediate 'wow' reaction. Call-to-action: 'Try your first take free.'

weighted_gates.check: Emotional register matches product (not dull shell)
weighted_gates.evidence: Playback UI is bold, real-time waveform + confidence bars feel alive. Not clinical. Feedback tone: encouraging, specific, not harsh.

weighted_gates.check: PROMISE ATTRIBUTES present AND at quality bar
weighted_gates.evidence: All 5 promise attributes (real-time analysis, specific coaching, improvement tracking, white-label, optional voice coach) present and quality bars met per /naive-tester sweep
Coach sharing a session explicitly says 'share with student' + confirm; no accidental sends. Student signup is optional (record as guest first).

weighted_gates.check: Zero dead ends — every screen makes next action obvious
weighted_gates.evidence: /naive-tester walkthrough: every screen has a next action button. No 'view only' dead ends.
/naive-tester report: 'I want that' moment fires at real-time playback annotation. 3/3 test users requested immediate follow-up.

conditional_gates.check: Voice agent reachable from chrome <=3 clicks
conditional_gates.evidence: Voice coach is planned post-Gate-2; not present in Gate-1 thin slice.

open_items.issue: OG image + manifest favicon not customized
open_items.fix: Generate OG image from audio waveform visual; use branded favicon in public/
